App.Title: Cloak of Darkness
App.Author: Roger Firth (spec), ported to Kitsoki
App.License: Spec in public domain; this port CC0

drop_cloak.Title: Drop the cloak
drop_cloak.Description: Drop the cloak on the floor wherever you're standing.

direction.FormatHint: One of n/s/e/w/up/down, spelled out or abbreviated.

hang_cloak.Title: Hang the cloak
hang_cloak.Description: Hang the cloak on the hook in the cloakroom.
Examples: hang my cloak on the hook

read_message.Title: Read the message
read_message.Description: Read the message scrawled in the sawdust on the bar floor.

bar.Description: A small bar off the foyer. Lit when the cloak is hung up; dark otherwise.

dark.Description: Too dark to see. Fumbling around increases disturbance.

body.Source: It's pitch dark in here. You can just make out a message on the floor, but can't read it without more light.

*.GuardHint: It's pitch dark -- you can't see to do that.

Effects.Say: Blundering around in the dark isn't a good idea.

go.GuardHint: You can't see well enough in the dark to head that way. Try going north, or finding a way to make light.

lit.Description: The bar, lit now the cloak is hung up. A message is scrawled on the floor.

body.Source: The bar, much rougher than you'd have guessed after the opulence of the foyer, is completely empty. There seems to be some sort of message scrawled in the sawdust on the floor.

Effects.Say: You can't go that way from the bar.

cloakroom.Description: A small cloakroom with a single hook. Exit east.

body.Source: The walls of this small room were clearly once lined with hooks, though now only one remains. The exit is a door to the east.
You are wearing a velvet cloak.
A velvet cloak hangs on the hook.

Effects.Say: You can't go that way from here.
You hang the cloak on the hook.

hang_cloak.GuardHint: You aren't wearing the cloak -- nothing to hang.

Effects.Say: You take the cloak from the hook.

body.Source:
The message, now little more than a dusty blur, appears to say:
"You have lost."

The message, neatly marked in the sawdust, reads:
"You have won."

foyer.Description: The entrance hall of the opera house. South leads to the bar; west to the cloakroom.

body.Source: You are in a spacious hall, splendidly decorated in red and gold, with glittering chandeliers overhead. The entrance from the street is to the north, and there are doorways south and west.

Effects.Say: You've only just arrived, and besides, the weather outside seems to be getting worse.

go.GuardHint: The weather outside is awful -- you've only just arrived.

OffPath.Banner: *** off the path -- responses do not affect your story ***

story.ask.Label: Ask about story
story.ask.Banner: Asking about this story — read-only, no edits.

story.bug.Label: Story bug
story.bug.Banner: Filing a story bug — write it down and the agent files it under issues/bugs/.

story.edit.Label: Edit story
story.edit.Banner: Editing this story's YAML — your changes affect the running app.

mermaid.source: %% Cloak of Darkness %% kitsoki viz --flowchart --detail states flowchart LR Start(["Start"]):::input subgraph SG_bar["phase 1 · bar — A small bar off the foyer. Lit when the cloak is hung up; dark otherwise."] direction LR ST_bar_dark[/"bar.dark"/]:::room ST_bar_lit[/"bar.lit"/]:::room end subgraph SG_cloakroom["phase 2 · cloakroom — A small cloakroom with a single hook. Exit east."] direction LR ST_cloakroom[/"cloakroom"/]:::room end subgraph SG_ended["phase 3 · ended — The journey is over."] direction LR ST_ended[/"ended"/]:::room end subgraph SG_foyer["phase 0 · foyer — The entrance hall of the opera house. South leads to the bar; west to the cloakroom."] direction LR ST_foyer[/"foyer"/]:::room end Start --> ST_foyer ST_bar_dark -- "*(any)" --> ST_bar_dark ST_bar_dark -- "go (default)" --> ST_bar_dark ST_bar_dark -- "go [slots.direction == '…]" --> ST_foyer ST_bar_lit -- "go (default)" --> ST_bar_lit ST_bar_lit -- "look" --> ST_bar_lit ST_bar_lit -- "read_message" --> ST_ended ST_bar_lit -- "go [slots.direction == '…]" --> ST_foyer ST_cloakroom -- "go (default)" --> ST_cloakroom ST_cloakroom -- "hang_cloak (default)" --> ST_cloakroom ST_cloakroom -- "hang_cloak [world.wearing_cloak …]" --> ST_cloakroom ST_cloakroom -- "look" --> ST_cloakroom ST_cloakroom -- "wear_cloak (default)" --> ST_cloakroom ST_cloakroom -- "wear_cloak [world.wearing_cloak …]" --> ST_cloakroom ST_cloakroom -- "go [slots.direction == '…]" --> ST_foyer ST_foyer -- "go [slots.direction == '…]" --> ST_bar ST_foyer -- "go [slots.direction == '…]" --> ST_cloakroom ST_foyer -- "go (default)" --> ST_foyer ST_foyer -- "go [slots.direction == '…]" --> ST_foyer ST_foyer -- "look" --> ST_foyer classDef input fill:#eef,stroke:#446,stroke-width:1.5px classDef room fill:#fff,stroke:#357,stroke-width:1.5px classDef work fill:#f7f7fa,stroke:#888 classDef llm fill:#f0e6ff,stroke:#6a3eb5,stroke-width:2px,color:#3a2270 classDef shell fill:#fef6e4,stroke:#a85,stroke-width:1.5px classDef store fill:#eef5fb,stroke:#369,stroke-width:1px classDef cache fill:#fff6e0,stroke:#a86,stroke-width:1.5px classDef err fill:#fff5f5,stroke:#c66,stroke-width:1px,stroke-dasharray:4 3 classDef next fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#272,stroke-width:1.5px classDef note fill:#fffbe6,stroke:#a90,stroke-width:1px,stroke-dasharray:2 2,color:#553

attrs.prompt: You are an interactive fiction engine running a game called Cloak of Darkness. The player is in the foyer — a spacious hall splendidly decorated in red and gold, with glittering chandeliers overhead. The entrance from the street is to the north, and there are doorways south and west. The player is wearing a velvet cloak. Intents available: go (move in a direction), look (look around). Your task is to determine which intent the player expresses and extract any slot values. Reply with a JSON object: {"intent": "<intent-name>", "slots": {}}. Only use the available intents listed. If the player's input does not clearly match any intent, use the closest one or ask for clarification. Remember: you are not the narrator — you are the intent router. Do not generate game text; only route the intent. Input: "look"

attrs.response: After reviewing the player's input carefully, I can identify this as a "look" intent — the player wants to examine or describe the current room. This is a straightforward intent match. {"intent": "look", "slots": {}}

attrs.banner: *** off the path -- responses do not affect your story ***

attrs.prompt: You are Claude, an AI assistant made by Anthropic. The player has engaged off-path mode in this interactive fiction game. In this mode you can respond freely without being constrained to the game's state machine. The player is asking you to introduce yourself. This is a meta-conversation outside the game narrative — you are free to be helpful and conversational. The player said: 'tell me about yourself'. Please respond naturally and helpfully, explaining what you are and how you're helping power this interactive fiction experience. You can mention that you're the AI helping route intents and generate responses in the Cloak of Darkness game, but you're also just Claude. Be warm, brief, and engaging.

You are an interactive fiction engine running a game called Cloak of Darkness. The player is in the foyer — a spacious hall splendidly decorated in red and gold, with glittering chandeliers overhead. The entrance from the street is to the north, and there are doorways south and west. The player is wearing a velvet cloak. Intents available: go (move in a direction), look (look around). Your task is to determine which intent the player expresses and extract any slot values. Reply with a JSON object: {"intent": "<intent-name>", "slots": {}}. Only use the available intents listed. If the player's input does not clearly match any intent, use the closest one or ask for clarification. Remember: you are not the narrator — you are the intent router. Do not generate game text; only route the intent. Input: "tell me about yourself"
attrs.response: I'm Claude, an AI assistant created by Anthropic. In this interactive fiction experience, I'm working behind the scenes to help interpret your natural language inputs — figuring out which game intent you mean (like 'go', 'look', 'hang the cloak') and extracting any relevant slot values (like which direction you want to go). I'm also helping to generate the narrative responses when you do things in the game world. Think of me as the intelligence that bridges your natural English with the structured state machine that powers the Cloak of Darkness story. Outside the game, I can chat with you freely about anything — this is exactly what off-path mode is for. The cloak game itself is a classic work of interactive fiction by Roger Firth, adapted here as a kitsoki state machine to demonstrate how the system works. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

attrs.error: I/O error writing session state to disk: no space left on device
